The fifteenth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet. Its sound differs from that of ر r by being uttered with the tip of the tongue turned upwards towards the roof of the mouth; and closely resembles that of ḍa, with which it is often interchanged. In reckoning by abjad it counts 200, the same as re. It rarely (in Hindī never) occurs as the first letter of a word, there being, so far as we have been able to discover, only one solitary vocable under this class.

adv. Every vein; in every vein; throughout the veins, from vein to vein

A vein to stand out or to swell

def: s.f. = رك rak, q.v.
s.m. (in Pros.), A Cretic (¯̆¯).
s.f. Rubbing against, rubbing, friction, attrition, abrasion, graze; the state of being worn; — the quick beating of a drum
subs: (-kī), To be rubbed (by), be abraded, be grazed, be worn; to rub (against, -se), to come into contact (with), to collide (with)
(-kī), To be rabbed (by), be abraded, be grazed.

def: s.m. Rubbing, grinding; scouring, polishing; — quarrelling, wrangling; — a kind of collyrium, an ointment for the eyes
subs: s.m. Debate, disputation, discussion, dissension, altercation, quarrel, wrangling, strife.

ety: ragaṛ˚, prob. = S. प्र+घृष्ट, rt. घृष्
def: v.t. To rub, rub down, grind, grate, fret, abrade, excoriate; to wear out; — to burnish, polish.
s.m. (dialec.) Blood.
s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: for A. رغبة, inf. n. of رغب 'to desire,' &c.
def: s.f. Desire, vehement desire, longing, avidity; wish; curiosity; inclination, disposition; esteem, affection, pleasure
subs: (-kī), To create, incite, or stimulate desire (of or for)
(-par, or -kī), To entertain desire (for), to long (for), set the heart (on)
(-kī t̤araf, or -par, or -kī), To evince desire (for), to long (for); to covet.

def: s.f. Pursuit, chase, chevy, hunt
subs: (-kī), To be in pursuit (of), to chase (for the purpose of pairing, — applied to pigeons and other animals).

ety: raged˚, prob. = S. प्र+खेदय(ति), rt. खिद्
def: v.t. To pursue, chase, chevy, hunt, drive off or away.
s.m. (f. -ī), A descendant of Raghu; a patronymic of Rām-ćandra; — sea, ocean; — a species of large fish, Silurus pelorius(?) — an intruding guest.
s.m. Name of an ancient king of Oude, great grandfather of Rām-ćandra; name of the offspring of this king (an abbreviation for raghu-vaṅśa, q.v.)
subs: raghu-rāj, raghu-rājā, raghu-rāʼī, s.m. = raghu-nāth, q.v.
s.m. The race or offspring of Raghu
s.m. 'The gem of the race of Raghu,' i.e. Rām-ćandra
raghu-nāyak, s.m. 'Lord of the race of Raghu,' an epithet of Daśarath, and of Rām
s.m. 'Descendant or son of Raghu,' an epithet of Rām
s.m. 'The best (or chief, or the eldest) of the race of Raghu,' an epithet of Rām
vulg. raghu-baṅs, s.m. The race or offspring of Raghu; — name of Kālidāsa's celebrated poem in honour of the race of Raghu
vulg. raghu-baṅsī, adj. & s.m. Of or relating to the family or race of Raghu; — a descendant of Raghu
s.m. 'The hero of the race of Raghu,' an epithet of Rām.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Coloured, dyed; imbued (with); full of passion or feeling, impassioned; passionate; loving; passionately found (of); — lustful, carnal, sensual; — a painter; a singer; a lover; a lustful man, libertine.
s.f. A sort of grain, Eleusine coracana (much cultivated in southern India).
adj. & s.m. Self-willed, intractable, hardened, persisting in an evil or vicious course or habit; — a self-willed person; one who is hardened in any particular vice or habit.
part. adj. Desiring, wishing (for); willing; desirous; eager; curious
subs: v.t. To make (one) desirous (of), to incite, encourage; to dispose, incline (to)
(-kā), To be inclined or disposed (to); to become eager (for), desire eagerly.
